headline: GLC board members hold average 4.7 directorships each
context: A corporate governance review found that directors of Malaysia's top 20 GLCs hold an average of 4.7 board seats each, raising concerns about oversight capacity and conflicts of interest.

cards.big: GLC directors juggle 4.7 board seats on average — who's actually watching?
cards.sub: Top 20 GLCs share a revolving door of 180 directors across 87 companies

cards.big: MCCG 2021 recommends maximum of 5 directorships per individual
cards.sub: 23% of GLC directors exceed this limit with some holding 8-9 positions

cards.big: Top 20 GLCs manage combined assets worth RM1.8 trillion
cards.sub: Overstretched directors risk rubber-stamping decisions on massive public wealth

cards.big: Singapore's Temasek limits board members to 3 external directorships maximum
cards.sub: Stricter limits ensure directors have capacity for meaningful governance oversight

cards.big: A director who sits on 7 boards is a name on letterhead, not a governance safeguard
Should GLC directors be limited to 3 board seats to ensure genuine oversight?
